revisedPrompt:
Create exactly one PNG image: a clean game-development pixel-art animated sprite sheet.

Canvas and sheet contract:
- Exact canvas size: 1024x512 px.
- Layout: 4 columns x 2 rows, 8 equal cells, each 256x256 px.
- Top row contains frames 1-4 left to right; bottom row contains frames 5-8 left to right.
- Do not draw visible grid lines, cell separators, borders, frame numbers, labels, text, logos, UI, or watermarks.
- Background must be one flat solid chroma key color #FF00FF filling every empty pixel; no gradients, no texture, no shadows on background.
- Each frame must stay fully inside its own 256x256 cell, with clear #FF00FF margin around body, sword, scabbard, hair, cords, streamers, and feet.
- No detached fragments, no dust specks, no isolated pixels, no leftover effects, no seam slivers.

Style:
- Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-plus SNES-style pixel art, no antialiasing blur, readable pixel clusters, consistent palette.
- One-pixel dark outline where useful, top-left light source.
- Bright cheerful mythical-Japan action RPG palette.
- Same character identity and same exact scale in every frame. Re-pose the character only; do not redesign, restyle, resize, or change proportions.

Reference matching:
- Match the attached approved walking-sheet character exactly in proportions, head-to-body ratio, slim-but-sturdy body width, palette, outline style, pixel density, clothing colors, and accessory shapes.
- This is the same hero Mito, now viewed from UP/back in top-down 3/4 view. Back view means show the back of the head, tiny low ponytail, and back of the indigo haori; do not show the face.
- Body height should be about 70% of cell height so sword extensions fit inside the cell with margin. Feet should align to the same ground line in every cell.

Character details to preserve:
- Androgynous 15-year-old wanderer, ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, vermilion braided-cord hair ornament.
- White tube-sleeved top under a short indigo-blue haori jacket, with small vermilion braided-cord knots on sleeves and hem.
- Loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties.
- Light travel sandals.
- Vermilion braided cord belt-sash with small tassel.
- Necklace with three pale-green magatama beads may be mostly hidden from back view.
- At left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword: white scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers. It must read as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana.
- No samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho.

Animation: one continuous IAI / battou-jutsu draw-cut motion, facing UP/back view, no motion trail, no swoosh, no afterimage, no energy effect. Draw only the solid character body, scabbard, and sword.

Frame poses:
1. IAI stance: knees slightly bent, body lowered and coiled, left hand gripping the white scabbard at left hip, right hand resting on the hilt, blade still fully sheathed.
2. Deeper crouch, maximum tension, still sheathed; charge-loop pose compatible with frame 1.
3. Draw begins: blade halfway out of the scabbard, body starting to twist, back view still clear.
4. Battou key pose: blade fully drawn in one lightning cut away from the viewer; arms extend forward/up and are partly hidden by body; blade visible above shoulder line; decisive pose, still fully contained in the cell.
5. Follow-through: swing decelerates past the body, tiny white paper streamers on hilt/scabbard trailing as solid attached shapes only.
6. Settling: weight forward, blade low, controlled stance.
7. Noto: guides blade back toward the scabbard mouth at left hip.
8. Sheathed again, calm stance, loops cleanly back to frame 1.

Hard avoid:
- No Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, or likenesses.
- No gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature, no letter emblems.
- No cell crossing, no floating detached sword pieces, no separate effects.
